questions.question: Who is eligible to connect Google Calendar to Notion AI?

options.text: Any Notion user with a Personal Plan
options.explanation: Personal plans do not have access to the connector; only Business or Enterprise plans do.

options.text: A Google Workspace admin who also owns the Notion workspace, on a Business or Enterprise plan
options.explanation: Both conditions are required: Google Workspace admin, Notion workspace owner, and a Business/Enterprise plan.

options.text: Only the Notion workspace owner, regardless of Google role
options.explanation: Being a Notion owner alone is insufficient; you must also be a Google Workspace admin.

options.text: Any member of the Google Workspace organization
options.explanation: Regular members cannot set up the integration; admin rights are required.

questions.question: After creating a new event in Google Calendar, how long does it typically take before Notion AI can search for it?

options.text: Immediately
options.explanation: There is a short propagation delay; it's not instant.

options.text: Within 30 minutes
options.explanation: The article states new events become searchable within 30 minutes.

options.text: After 24 hours
options.explanation: 24 hours is the deletion window after disconnection, not the search delay.

options.text: Only after a manual refresh in Notion
options.explanation: While a refresh may be needed to see the connector, the search delay is still ~30 minutes.

questions.question: Which of the following locations does NOT support searching Google Calendar via the Notion AI connector?

options.text: The Notion AI chat in the bottom‑right corner
options.explanation: This location does support the connector.

options.text: Notion AI in the sidebar
options.explanation: This location also supports the connector.

options.text: Search in the sidebar
options.explanation: Sidebar search can use the connector as well.

options.text: The Notion page editor toolbar
options.explanation: The toolbar is not listed as a place where the Google Calendar connector can be used.

questions.question: During the connection setup, which installation scope is recommended for the best Notion AI answers?

options.text: Install for the individual connecting user only
options.explanation: Installing for a single user limits the data Notion AI can draw from.

options.text: Install for All users in the organization
options.explanation: The guide recommends installing for all users to improve answer quality.

options.text: Install for a random subset of users
options.explanation: There is no benefit to a random subset; the recommendation is for all users.

options.text: Do not install; the connector works without installation
options.explanation: Installation via the Marketplace app is required for the connector to function.

questions.question: If you disconnect Google Calendar from Notion AI, when is the associated data permanently deleted?

options.text: Immediately, as soon as you click Disconnect
options.explanation: Data becomes unsearchable immediately, but deletion occurs later.

options.text: Within 24 hours of the disconnection request
options.explanation: The article states all associated data is deleted within 24 hours after disconnect.

options.text: After 7 days, to allow for recovery
options.explanation: There is no 7‑day retention mentioned.

options.text: Only when the Notion workspace is deleted
options.explanation: Data is deleted independently of workspace deletion.

questions.question: Which of the following prompts would be least appropriate for the Google Calendar AI Connector?

options.text: "When is my next meeting with Richard?"
options.explanation: This directly asks about a calendar event, which is appropriate.

options.text: "Brief me on my schedule for next week."
options.explanation: A valid request for a weekly overview from the calendar.

options.text: "Summarize the latest design mockups in Figma."
options.explanation: The connector only accesses Google Calendar data, not Figma files.

options.text: "What were the dates that I was in Seoul last month?"
options.explanation: If travel was entered as calendar events, this is a valid query.

questions.question: How does Notion AI ensure that one user cannot see another user’s private Google Calendar events?

options.text: It aggregates all events and then filters them per user after the search
options.explanation: Aggregating first would expose data before filtering.

options.text: Each user’s AI search is limited to calendar entries that match their exact email address
options.explanation: The article explains that searches are scoped by exact email match.

options.text: Notion AI stores a copy of every user’s calendar and enforces permissions locally
options.explanation: Notion does not store calendar data long‑term.

options.text: All users share a single service account, but Notion masks private events
options.explanation: A shared account would break privacy guarantees.

questions.question: What temporary storage mechanism does Notion use for caching Google Calendar content to speed up searches?

options.text: A vector database like Turbopuffer
options.explanation: The article explicitly says Notion does NOT use vector databases for calendar data.

options.text: Memcached or a similar in‑memory cache
options.explanation: Notion may temporarily cache data via something like memcached.

options.text: Long‑term storage in Notion’s relational database
options.explanation: Calendar data is not stored long‑term in Notion's databases.

options.text: Local browser storage on the user’s device
options.explanation: Caching occurs on Notion’s servers, not client‑side storage.

questions.question: A user who is a Notion workspace member but not a Google Workspace admin attempts to connect Google Calendar. What will happen?

options.text: The connection will succeed, but only for that user’s calendar
options.explanation: Admin rights are required; the connection will not succeed.

options.text: The connection will be blocked because both admin and workspace‑owner privileges are required
options.explanation: Both conditions must be met; lacking admin rights prevents the setup.

options.text: Notion AI will automatically elevate the user to admin status
options.explanation: No automatic privilege escalation occurs.

options.text: The user can connect, but only after a manual approval from an existing admin
options.explanation: The article does not mention a manual approval workflow; admin rights are mandatory upfront.

questions.question: If a Google Workspace user’s email address changes, how does Notion AI handle their access to the Google Calendar connector?

options.text: The user retains access until they manually re‑authenticate with the new email
options.explanation: Access is tied to exact email matching, not manual re‑auth.

options.text: Notion automatically syncs permission changes, revoking access for the old email and granting it to the new one
options.explanation: The system syncs permission changes from Google Workspace immediately.

options.text: The connector continues to work with the old email until the admin disconnects and reconnects it
options.explanation: Permission sync is immediate; no need for manual reconnection.

options.text: Access is unaffected because Notion stores a permanent copy of the calendar data
options.explanation: Notion does not store calendar data long‑term; access depends on current permissions.
